name: Spaghetti Squash Florentine
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces frozen chopped spinach, thawed and drained
1/2 cup part skim milk mozzarella cheese, shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. STOVETOP: In a 6-quart Dutch oven over high heat, in boiling water, cook the spaghetti squash for 20 minutes or until fork-tender. Remove from water. Halve the squash lengthwise; remove seeds.
MICROWAVE: Halve the squash lengthwise. Scrape out seeds. Place cut-side down on a microwave-safe dish and cook for about 20 minutes on HIGH or until fork-tender, turning over once and covering with wax paper.
Cool the squash slightly and use a fork to separate into strands. Set aside.
2. In a medium bowl, combine ricotta cheese, spinach, egg beaters, and seasonings; mix well. Spread evenly in a 13x9-inch baking dish coated with cooking spray. Spread Parmesan and nonfat mozzarella cheeses over the spinach mixture.
3. In a large bowl, combine the squash and spaghetti sauce; toss to coat well. Arrange the squash mixture on the spinach mixture; top with part skim milk mozzarella cheese. Cover with foil; bake at 375°F for 30 minutes or until hot and bubbly. Makes 6 servings.
Per serving: 220 Kcal, 5g fat (2g sat fat), 20% CFF, 628mg Na, 8mg cholesterol.
